parts.stem:
The diagram shows a Coolidge tube used for the production of X-rays. X-rays are emitted in the directions shown by the arrows when a beam of electrons strikes the point from which the arrows originate.

Which of the following most correctly describe the reason for using the angle of the target surface relative to the electron beam that strikes the surface? Select all that apply.

choices.label: A
choices.value: The angle of the surface is used to control the direction in which X-rays are most strongly emitted from the target material.

choices.label: B
choices.value: The angle of the surface is used to increase the area on the target that electrons are incident on.

choices.label: C
choices.value: The angle of the surface is used to control the rate of production of X-rays in the target material.

correct_answer.label: A
correct_answer.value: The angle of the surface is used to control the direction in which X-rays are most strongly emitted from the target material.

correct_answer.label: B
correct_answer.value: The angle of the surface is used to increase the area on the target that electrons are incident on.

parts.explanation: The angle of the surface is used to control the direction in which X-rays are most strongly emitted from the target material. The angle of the surface is used to increase the area on the target that electrons are incident on.
